summary: n8n community node exfiltrates credential store and host recon to hardcoded remote endpoint on load
details: Pwn.node.js runs shell commands synchronously at module load: it reads /etc/hosts, runs ifconfig, nslookup, ARP and TCP table enumeration, and cats /home/node/.n8n/database.sqlite (n8n's credential and workflow store) piped through `strings | grep -i -E "flag|password|secret"` along with /home/node/.n8n/config. The concatenated output is written to /tmp/.pwnout3 and POSTed via http.request to the hardcoded endpoint http://10.0.0.145:8000/rce-out3. The n8n SQLite database contains stored workflow credentials and secrets, so this is direct harvesting of installer-side secrets and host reconnaissance sent to a hardcoded non-first-party destination with no caller configuration or opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
